📈 Compound Interest Calculator

Calculate how your savings or investment grows over time with the power of compound interest.

Enter your investment details

What is compound interest?

Compound interest is when the interest you earn is added to your principal, so that interest also earns interest. Over time, this creates exponential growth. Albert Einstein reportedly called it the eighth wonder of the world.

Frequently asked questions

What is a realistic annual return?

Historically, global stock markets have returned an average of 7–10% annually before inflation. Savings accounts and bonds typically offer lower returns.

How often does this calculator compound?

This calculator compounds monthly, which is the most common frequency for investment funds and savings accounts.

What is the difference between simple and compound interest?

With simple interest you only earn interest on your principal. With compound interest, your interest also earns interest, creating much greater growth over time.
